evidence_excerpt: After rituximab, MF showed a sustained improvement (noted at 3–6 months and over a follow-up period of 1 year), while KD lesions persisted in size though became softer and less nodular.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence: Post-treatment, MF improved and KD lesions persisted but were softer/less nodular; no activity limitations or systemic complications reported, suggesting at most minor functional impact from residual cutaneous disease.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence: Follow-up reported persistent KD lesions (same size but softer/less nodular) with sustained improvement of MF; indicates ongoing but less prominent cutaneous disease rather than complete resolution.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: While on therapy, she experienced a mild transient increase in the number of her MF lesions.
Follow-up at 3 months after the end of treatment revealed significant and sustained improvement of the MF lesions.
At 6 months, MF lesions remained significantly improved; KD lesions were noted to be the same size but significantly softer and less nodular upon palpation; serum IgE dropped to 222 IU/L.
MF exhibited a sustained improvement over a follow-up period of 1 year.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact date of definitive diagnosis and treatment planning is not provided; diagnosis of KD and MF with subsequent decision for rituximab was used as the reference. Long-term data beyond 1 year are not available. Functional status and symptom burden are inferred from post-treatment clinical descriptions of residual lesions and improvements, as explicit activity or symptom assessments were not detailed.
